news.summary: A new product called Gushers All Blue has been launched by Gushers, in response to the strong demand from fans for the blue flavors. This new product is the first-ever box to contain only blue Gushers and is now available nationwide. The Gushers All Blue pack includes the popular Blueberry Grape flavor as well as a new flavor, Sour Blue Raspberry. This release comes after years of fans expressing their love for the blue Gushers, leading the company to finally make their dreams a reality. The product is set to hit shelves across the country this month.
news.headline: Gushers launches long-awaited All Blue Pack due to popular demand, with Blueberry Grape and Sour Blue Raspberry flavors.

news.summary: In a recent report, the chief economist at RSM, Joseph Brusuelas, has raised concerns about the possibility of a recession triggered by tariffs, with warnings that it could potentially originate from the coastal region, consequently leading to a rise in inflation and unemployment rates. Brusuelas highlighted the significance of consistent tariff policies, cautioning that any disruptions could spark a recession that may start from the Los Angeles docks. He emphasized that approximately 22% of jobs in the United States are linked to transportation, warehousing, and trade sectors, underscoring the importance of monitoring import container volumes at ports like Los Angeles and Long Beach as crucial indicators of economic health. Brusuelas criticized President Donald Trump's tariff strategies as a form of "misapplied consumption tax," expressing fears that these measures could prematurely end the ongoing economic growth. He anticipates that the adverse effects of such policies will be initially centered around port operations before spreading across the wider economy. The detailed analysis can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Economist warns of potential tariff-induced recession starting at Port of Los Angeles, affecting 22% of US jobs tied to transportation.

news.summary: Dublin - A new report titled "Industry Insights: The Impact of Tariffs on Consumer Packaged Goods" is now available on ResearchAndMarkets.com. The Trump Administration's actions have included the imposition or potential imposition of tariffs on exports from nearly all countries globally. The report offers a comprehensive analysis of how these tariffs are affecting the consumer packaged goods industry worldwide, highlighting potential winners and losers. Particularly, tariffs enforced by the US are of significant importance in shaping the sector's landscape.
news.headline: Report analyzes impact of tariffs on Consumer Packaged Goods sector globally due to US imposing or threatening tariffs on exports from almost all nations. Identifies winners and losers.

news.summary: Shares of Bunge Limited (NYSE: BG) are on the rise during premarket trading following the release of their first-quarter results. The company reported adjusted earnings per share for the quarter at $1.81, surpassing analysts' expectations of $1.29. However, quarterly sales of $11.64 billion, marking a 13% decrease from the previous year, fell short of the projected $13.05 billion. Bunge's Chief Executive, Greg Heckman, noted, "We saw benefits in the first quarter due to tariff-related timing shifts in demand and farmer activity, and we maintain confidence in our ability to perform well despite the current market conditions." More details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Bunge beats Q1 EPS expectation at $1.81, missing sales estimate of $11.64 billion; CEO attributes success to tariff shifts.

news.summary: Monster Beverage (MNST) is set to report its quarterly earnings on May 8, with analysts expecting an EPS of $0.46. Investors are anticipating positive news that could potentially exceed estimates and provide optimistic guidance for the next quarter. Past performance shows that last quarter, MNST missed EPS by $0.02, resulting in a 5.26% increase in share price the following day. The stock is currently trading at $60.02, with a 52-week increase of 8.79%. Analysts rate Monster Beverage as a Buy, with an average price target of $61.11, suggesting a 1.82% upside. It is essential for investors to consider future projections as they heavily impact stock prices and market sentiments.
news.headline: Monster Beverage (NASDAQ: MNST) to announce Q1 2024 earnings soon. Analysts estimate EPS at $0.46. Stock priced at $60.02, up 8.79% in 52 weeks. Consensus rating is Buy with $61.11 price target. Market awaits if the company will surpass estimates.

news.summary: In Tempe, Arizona on May 7, 2025, it was reported that economic activity within the hospital subsector showed growth in April for the 20th consecutive month. This growth came after experiencing contraction twice within the previous four-month period. Prior to this, there were 35 consecutive months of growth in the sector. The information was provided by the nation's hospital supply executives.

news.summary: In the latest report by the ActivTrak Productivity Lab, it was disclosed that workers in the logistics industry are at the forefront of adopting artificial intelligence (AI) technologies. According to the study conducted in Austin, Texas on May 7, 2025, it was revealed that employees in the logistics sector have the longest workday, spanning approximately 9 hours and 10 minutes. This duration surpasses the average workday across all industries by 26 minutes. These statistics underline the growing integration of AI in the logistics field and suggest a trend towards longer working hours in this particular sector.
news.headline: Logistics workers have longest workday at 9hrs, 10 mins, 26 mins above average; highest burnout risk and AI adoption.

news.summary: Economist Mohamed El-Erian has forecasted that the Federal Reserve will likely maintain the current interest rates amidst global economic uncertainty and escalating trade tensions with China. The Allianz Chief Economic Advisor stated that the Federal Reserve, which heavily relies on data, would probably have difficulty justifying any changes to interest rates in the upcoming meeting. El-Erian pointed out the divergence in approaches expected in central bank meetings scheduled for the week, such as an anticipated rate cut by the UK and a potential hike by Brazil. He also emphasized the significance of forthcoming economic data that could reflect the impact of the increasing trade tensions between the U.S. and China, particularly focusing on China's Caixin PMI index and to a lesser extent on manufacturing outputs in Europe. This analysis is available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Economist El-Erian expects Fed to keep current rates due to global uncertainty, warns of US-China trade impact on key economic data. Fed likely to maintain rates amid global economic uncertainty and growing trade tensions with China; upcoming data to reflect impact on private sector activity and factory output.

news.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority has revealed a tender for RMB Institutional Government Bonds with a maturity of 10 years. The total value of these bonds being offered is RMB1.0 billion. The tender is scheduled to take place on May 13, 2025.
news.headline: Hong Kong to auction RMB1.0 billion 10-year Institutional Govt Bonds on May 13, 2025, announced by HKMA.

news.summary: U.S. stock futures dropped following a nine-day winning streak for the S&P 500 index, with major benchmark indices lower in premarket trading. President Trump confirmed U.S. officials are in trade discussions with China, but no meeting with President Xi Jinping is planned. Chinese proposed special envoys for negotiations, which the U.S. is hesitant about, preferring direct talks between the leaders. Investors await the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision, with markets pricing a 98.2% likelihood of rates remaining unchanged. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.31% and the two-year was at 3.82%. Futures saw changes: Dow Jones -0.66%, S&P 500 -0.77%, Nasdaq 100 -0.89%, Russell 2000 -0.88%. ETFs tracking the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 fell in premarket. The S&P 500, Dow, and Nasdaq saw gains for the week, the S&P 500 out of a correction zone, down 7.5% from its high. Dow Jones was 8.33% lower and Nasdaq 100 was 9.54% lower than their respective 52-week highs. Strong job data and factory orders lifted the market sentiment. Nonfarm payrolls exceeded forecasts at 177,000.
news.headline: US stock futures fall after S&P 500's 9-day winning streak; SPY down 0.70% to $562.82, QQQ down 0.84% to $484.74. Fed likely to keep interest rates unchanged with markets pricing at 98.2% chance. Dow -0.66%, S&P 500 -0.77%, Nasdaq 100 -0.89%, Russell 2000 -0.88%. Markets rebounded last week: S&P up 2.9%, Dow up 3%, Nasdaq up 3.4%. S&P 500 out of correction zone, down 7.5% from record high. Jobs data and factory orders boosted market. Trump confirms US-China trade talks ongoing, no planned Xi meeting this week.

news.summary: TPG Inc. (NASDAQ: TPG) stock prices have decreased today following the announcement of their acquisition of Peppertree Capital Management, Inc. The deal, valued at up to $660 million, will be settled with a mix of cash and equity. The cash portion is set at around $242 million, while the equity component is expected to reach up to $418 million upon completion, with adjustments applicable. Additionally, there is a potential for earnout payments totaling up to $300 million contingent on Peppertree's future performance. The agreement has received approval from TPG's Board of Directors and is slated to finalize in the near future. In a separate transaction, Sabre has divested its Hospitality Arm to TPG for $1.1 billion, with plans to reduce debt.
news.headline: TPG acquires Peppertree for up to $660 million in cash and equity deal. Earnouts may reach $300 million based on performance.

news.summary: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority has revealed plans to launch a tender for RMB 1.5 billion worth of 2-year government bonds as a component of the Infrastructure Bond Programme. The auction is slated for May 13, 2025.
news.headline: Hong Kong set for RMB 1.5 billion government bonds tender on May 13, 2025, under Infrastructure Bond Programme.

news.summary: Financial markets are pricing in rapid interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve, suggesting a cycle starting in July. However, short-term rate market data differs, indicating a potential shift. Chief Investment Officer Bob Elliott of Unlimited Funds believes current economic indicators support maintaining the current stance under Chair Jerome Powell, with stable unemployment rates being a key factor. With slowing labor force growth due to immigration restrictions, fewer than 100,000 new jobs per month may be sufficient to sustain this stability. Despite weak GDP, Elliott anticipates forward growth expectations of over 2% for the remainder of 2025, giving the Fed little urgency to cut rates. He suggests that aggressive cuts are unlikely without a significant deterioration of financial conditions in the near term. For more information, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Market expects swift Fed interest rate cuts; expert predicts future chair could start cycle. Unemployment key factor, GDP expected to grow over 2% despite recent weakness. Fed likely to wait for significant worsening of financial conditions before cutting rates.

The insights shared highlighted the significant impact it's having on the industry, showcasing how this technology is reshaping the future of finance. The event emphasized the potential of XRP Ledger in propelling DeFi forward and its role in driving advancements in the sector. This conference shed light on the intricate workings of this system, illustrating its potential applications in the real world. These developments underscore the growing importance of XRP Ledger as a disruptor in the financial realm and a catalyst for change in the sector.
news.headline: XRP Ledger reshaping DeFi with stablecoin advancements in real-world finance, per Chainalysis Links.  #Ripple #DeFi

news.summary: The annual Milken Institute conference has brought together prominent figures from Wall Street and key officials of the Trump administration. A major focus of discussions at the conference will be President Trump's management of the U.S. financial markets. This gathering is significant due to the involvement of influential leaders and voices in finance and politics.
news.headline: Wall Street luminaries and officials convene at Milken Institute's conference in Beverly Hills to discuss Trump's impact on markets.

news.summary: Chaska, Minnesota, May 5, 2025 - Mike Lindell Media Corporation (OTC: MLMC) disclosed the appointment of attorney Daniel J. Eastman as General Counsel, effective immediately. Bringing more than 35 years of legal and regulatory expertise to the role, Mr. Eastman will supervise the Company's legal matters, corporate governance, and risk management strategies amid a surge in litigation activity and increased industry scrutiny. Mr. Eastman's background encompasses roles such as an SEC legal staff intern, state securities regulator, and practicing corporate law with a focus on Federal and state securities law compliance, as well as securities fraud defense. He holds extensive experience as a CEO, serving on corporate boards, and providing counsel on numerous intricate financial restructurings, both in public and private sectors. Noted for his adept handling of legal challenges, safeguarding shareholder value, and reinforcing corporate adherence in dynamic, high-stakes environments.
news.headline: Mike Lindell Media Corp. appoints Daniel J. Eastman as General Counsel with 35 years legal experience, OTC: MLMC.

news.summary: China and the U.S. are considering ending their tit-for-tat tariffs to focus on a potential trade agreement, leading Wall Street investor Ed Yardeni to lessen the likelihood of a U.S. recession from 45% to 35%. There are signs that both countries are looking to ease trade tensions, sparking optimism and a recent surge in the markets. Yardeni believes that a potential suspension of tariffs could be on the horizon as negotiations progress, causing markets to gain confidence in a resolution. This shift in trade dynamics has led to a more positive outlook, with Yardeni attributing the recent market rebound to this newfound optimism. The full article on Benzinga.com delves deeper into these developments.
news.headline: Wall Street veteran lowers recession risk to 35% as US-China trade war cools, hopeful for trade deal amidst tariff pause.

news.summary: Howard Hughes Holdings Inc. saw an increase in premarket trading as they revealed a deal with Pershing Square to invest $900 million in exchange for 9,000,000 new HHH shares. Bill Ackman's Pershing Square is acquiring HHH shares at a rate of $100 per share, a 48% premium over the previous closing price on May 2, 2025. Post-investment, Pershing Square will own 46.9% of HHH's outstanding shares, with agreed limits of 40% voting power and 47% beneficial ownership. This move allows HHH to expand as a holding company, gaining controlling interests in operating firms while maintaining its real estate focus. For further details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Pershing Square invests $900M in Howard Hughes, acquiring 9M shares at $100 each, 48% premium, owning 46.9%, capping voting power at 40%.

news.summary: Securities Litigation Partner, James (Josh) Wilson of Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP, is urging investors who experienced losses in Treace Medical to reach out to him directly. If you bought or gained securities in Treace Medical within the period of May 8, 2023, to May 7, 2024, and are interested in exploring your options, Wilson is available for consultation. Connect with him to discuss your situation.
news.headline: Law firm Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is investigating claims for investors of Treace Medical from May 8, 2023, to May 7, 2024. Contact them if affected.

news.summary: Women in Web3 are advocating for clarity, education, and community to attract more women into the crypto space. At the Blockchain Baddies event during Token2049 in Dubai, women emphasized the importance of simplifying technical concepts and creating safe spaces for learning. Providing clarity and building skills were highlighted as essential for making the industry more accessible to women. Women in the Web3 industry stressed the need to demystify crypto's vocabulary and increase financial literacy to attract more women and individuals into the space. Initiatives like SheFi play a crucial role in expanding female participation. Companies, such as Web3 financial platform Fasset, are actively seeking input from women-centric communities to improve their products and make crypto more accessible to women. Financial literacy and understanding Web3's concepts of ownership, freedom, and community were identified as key factors in attracting more people to the industry.
news.headline: Women in Web3 are advocating for more clarity, education, and community to increase female participation in crypto. Initiatives like SheFi help demystify the industry. Companies like Fasset are partnering with women-focused communities to improve products. Magazine feature on Ethereum's Pectra fork launch. Financial literacy is crucial for attracting more people to Web3.
